19.text: Functional API for softmax, cross-entropy, etc.

21.text: Pin both seeds for reproducible comparison across MHA/GQA/MQA variants.

45.text: Same small model config as previous labs — differences will come from KV head sharing.

46.text: 4 query heads — this stays constant across all three variants.

47.text: Single layer keeps the comparison focused on attention architecture.

55.text: RMSNorm — same lightweight normalization as lab 10.

61.text: Normalize by root-mean-square, no learned parameters.

64.text: The star of this lab: one attention class that handles MHA, GQA, and MQA by varying n_kv_head.

67.text: The only parameter that changes between variants: how many KV heads to use.

70.text: Query heads must divide evenly into KV groups — each group of query heads shares one KV head.

71.text: How many query heads share each KV head. MHA: 1 (no sharing). GQA: 2. MQA: 4 (all share one).

74.text: Queries always have the full n_head heads — query capacity is never reduced.

76.text: Keys use fewer heads — this is where the memory saving comes from. With MQA, this is just 1 * head_dim = 4 parameters per position instead of 4 * head_dim = 16.

77.text: Values match keys — always the same number of KV heads. The KV cache stores these, so fewer heads = less memory during inference.

79.text: Output projection is always full-sized — it recombines all query heads.

81.text: Forward pass — project, expand KV if needed, attend.

82.text: Unpack batch, sequence length, embedding dimension.

85.text: Queries: always full heads. Shape: (B, n_head, T, head_dim).

86.text: Keys: only n_kv_head heads. With MQA this is (B, 1, T, head_dim) — 4x smaller than queries.

87.text: Values: same reduced shape as keys.

90.text: If KV heads < query heads, we need to expand KV to match. This is the GQA/MQA trick.

91.text: Repeat each KV head to serve its group of query heads. Logically shared, but physically copied for the matmul. In practice, this repeat is nearly free — it's a view/broadcast, not a memcpy.

95.text: Standard scaled dot-product attention — identical for all three variants after KV expansion.

96.text: Causal mask: no peeking at future tokens.

100.text: Weighted sum of values, reshape back to (B, T, C).

114.text: Transformer block parameterized by KV head count.

115.text: Pass KV head count through to attention.

122.text: Forward: residual connections around attention and MLP.

128.text: Full model — takes n_kv_head to configure the attention variant.

129.text: KV head count flows from model to block to attention.

134.text: Stack of transformer blocks, all with the same KV head configuration.

139.text: Init function applied to all modules.

156.text: Run on CPU — fine for this tiny model.

160.text: Three variants to compare, all using the same model class with different n_kv_head values.

161.text: MHA: n_kv_head=4 (same as n_head) — every query head gets its own K,V. The standard approach from labs 01-08.

162.text: GQA: n_kv_head=2 — two groups of two query heads, each group sharing one K,V pair. The sweet spot used by LLaMA-2 70B.

163.text: MQA: n_kv_head=1 — all query heads share a single K,V pair. Maximum compression, used by PaLM and Falcon.

166.text: Shared generation function for all variants.

174.text: Feed current sequence through the model.

179.text: Stop if BOS is generated (end of name).

186.text: Train each variant in sequence with identical data and seeds.

192.text: Reset seeds before each variant for a fair comparison — same random init and data order.

196.text: Create model with this variant's KV head count.

197.text: Count total parameters — fewer KV heads = fewer KV projection parameters.

201.text: KV cache memory estimate — the key metric for inference. Fewer KV heads = proportionally less cache memory. At 7B scale with 2048 context, this difference is gigabytes per request.

231.text: Generate sample names from this variant.

239.text: Summary table: show how KV sharing affects parameter count and cache size.

240.text: Calculate KV projection parameters (wk + wv weights) for each variant.

241.text: Calculate KV cache memory in bytes (fp16 inference).
